ddls:
CREATE TABLE "webpages" (
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the webpage */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the webpage */,
  "page_power" INTEGER /* SEO score of the webpage */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the webpage in bytes */,
  "words" INTEGER /* Word count of the webpage */,
  "modified" TEXT /* Timestamp when the webpage was last modified */,
  "match_words" INTEGER /* Number of matching words for targeted keywords */,
  "match_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of matching words for targeted keywords */,
  "match_pages" INTEGER /* Number of pages with matching keywords */,
  "match_words_common" INTEGER /* Number of matching words for common keywords */,
  "match_percentage_common" REAL /* Percentage of matching words for common keywords */,
  "match_pages_common" INTEGER /* Number of pages with matching common keywords */,
  "broken_links_out" INTEGER /* Number of broken outgoing links from the webpage */,
  "links_out" INTEGER /* Number of outgoing links from the webpage */,
  "links_in" INTEGER /* Number of incoming links to the webpage */,
  "skip_reason" TEXT /* Reason why the webpage was skipped from analysis */,
  "redirected_to" TEXT /* URL of the page the webpage was redirected to */,
  "domain" TEXT /* Domain name of the client's website */,
  "content_type" TEXT /* Type of content (e.g., blog, product, about us) */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags for content categorization and filtering */,
  "seo_title" TEXT /* SEO title of the webpage */,
  "seo_description" TEXT /* SEO meta description of the webpage */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Timestamp when the webpage was last checked for SEO metrics */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("url")
)

CREATE TABLE "clients" (
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each client */,
  "client_name" TEXT /* Name of the client */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the client */,
  "website" TEXT /* Main website URL of the client */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the client */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the client account was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the client account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "seo_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each SEO metric record */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the webpage */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Targeted keyword for SEO */,
  "search_volume" INTEGER /* Search volume for the keyword */,
  "competition" REAL /* Competition level for the keyword */,
  "cpc" REAL /* Cost per click for the keyword */,
  "position" INTEGER /* Current position of the webpage for the keyword */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the SEO metrics were last updated */,
  "mobile_friendly" INTEGER /* Indicates if the webpage is optimized for mobile devices */,
  "page_speed" REAL /* Load time of the webpage in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_seo_metrics_url FOREIGN KEY ("url") REFERENCES webpages ("url")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., client, admin) */,
  "client_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the client the user belongs to */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_client_id FOREIGN KEY ("client_id") REFERENCES clients ("client_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the webpage */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the webpage accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the webpage was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser and device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_url FOREIGN KEY ("url") REFERENCES webpages ("url")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content update */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the webpage updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the update */,
  "update_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the update was made */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Detailed description of the changes made */,
  "reviewed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reviewed the update */,
  "review_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the content update was reviewed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_updates_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_updates_url FOREIGN KEY ("url") REFERENCES webpages ("url"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_content_updates_reviewed_by FOREIGN KEY ("reviewed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keyword_research" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword for SEO research */,
  "search_volume" INTEGER /* Search volume for the keyword */,
  "competition" REAL /* Competition level for the keyword */,
  "cpc" REAL /* Cost per click for the keyword */,
  "recommended" INTEGER /* Whether the keyword is recommended for use */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the keyword data was last updated */,
  "keyword_intent" TEXT /* Intent behind the keyword (e.g., informational, navigational, transactional) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "broken_links" (
  "link_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each broken link */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the webpage with the broken link */,
  "broken_url" TEXT /* URL of the broken link */,
  "discovered_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the broken link was discovered */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the broken link (e.g., reported, fixed, ignored) */,
  "fixed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who fixed the broken link */,
  "fixed_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the broken link was fixed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("link_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_broken_links_url FOREIGN KEY ("url") REFERENCES webpages ("url"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_broken_links_fixed_by FOREIGN KEY ("fixed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
